sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current state quickly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'N', 'W', 'G', 'b'):
        print(f"color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({min(rows)},{min(cols)},{max(rows)},{max(cols)})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The cross's right edge is at col 25, which is 3 columns to the left of the gap's left edge (col 28).
In level 1, the cross ended up with its right edge 1 column to the left of the gap's left edge. Here, the cross's right edge is 3 columns to the left.
Let me try clicking the bottom blue block more times to see if the cross can move further right.

Actually, looking at the current state, the middle white is gone (no white region at rows 24-39). The bottom white is at cols 0-19. The cross is at cols 22-25.
Let me try clicking the bottom blue block again to see if the cross can move further right.
